question_text: A student lifts a 2 kg mass to a height of 5 metres. Calculate the gravitational potential energy gained by the mass. (Use g = 10 m/s²)
explanation: Use the formula GPE = mass × gravity × height. GPE = 2 × 10 × 5 = 100 Joules. This is the energy stored in the gravitational potential energy store of the lifted mass.
